subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Skill World design crew to use solve elapsed time, measurement, mass, volume, graph, and line plot problems in real classroom missions.
lesson.mini_lesson: Solve elapsed time, measurement, mass, volume, graph, and line plot problems.
lesson.objective: Solve elapsed time, measurement, mass, volume, graph, and line plot problems.
lesson.concept_explanation: Use models and precise math language to solve elapsed time, measurement, mass, volume, graph, and line plot problems. Explain the strategy and include units when the answer needs them.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may subtract clock numbers without counting across the hour.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help with schedules, materials, maps, art projects, and data from everyday activities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the task, choose the model, solve carefully, and check that the answer matches the question.
hints: Read what the question asks.
Use the visual model to organize the information.
Check that your answer includes the right unit or name.

misconception_bank.elapsed_time_confusion: Learners may subtract clock numbers without counting across the hour.
misconception_bank.unit_conversion_confusion: Learners may mix grams, kilograms, liters, and milliliters.
misconception_bank.graph_scale_confusion: Learners may count bars instead of using the graph scale.
misconception_bank.line_plot_fraction_error: Learners may read fractional marks as whole numbers.

game_theme.title: Skill World: Time, Measurement, and Data
game_theme.mission_intro: Build confidence with time, measurement, and data through a guided mission and focused practice levels.

story.text: Listen to this page. This mission introduces measurement, time, and data. Look at units, labels, clocks, and graph scales in the picture. Think about how the mission story connects to the math. Match your answer to the unit and use child-friendly elapsed-time steps as you get ready. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches measurement, time, and data. Look at units, labels, clocks, and graph scales and read the vocabulary words. Match your answer to the unit and use child-friendly elapsed-time steps one step at a time. Watch out for forgetting the unit or reading the graph scale too quickly. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page shows a worked example for Time, Measurement, and Data. Look at the model and listen for how each part matches the question. Step one, read what is being asked. Step two, use the visual model. Step three, check the final answer with the right units or math words. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo lets you practice measurement, time, and data with help. Look at units, labels, clocks, and graph scales before you choose an answer. Match your answer to the unit and use child-friendly elapsed-time steps and explain your thinking in math words. Do not rush past forgetting the unit or reading the graph scale too quickly. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Use the model to help you work on measurement, time, and data. Match your answer to the unit and use child-friendly elapsed-time steps and check your answer. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. The problem may need careful thinking about measurement, time, and data. Look at units, labels, clocks, and graph scales, then decide which strategy fits best. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show what you know about measurement, time, and data. Read the problem, use the visual, and check for forgetting the unit or reading the graph scale too quickly. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose or write the answer when ready.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your work on Time, Measurement, and Data. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes helped your brain grow when you checked them. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows how your mission went. Look at your accuracy, hints, and growth notes. If a level needs more practice, go back to the hub or continue to Skill Practice. Use your results to choose what to practice next.
